quoted.text:
The Hormuz Escalation Cycle Keeps Repeating 🛢️⚠️

The pattern is simple

A trigger event hits shipping or military assets

Then the cycle accelerates

🔹 Iran or proxy forces hit a ship
🔹 The US strikes IRGC-linked targets
🔹 Iran responds with missiles, drones, cyber operations or proxy attacks
🔹 Gulf states and energy infrastructure become exposed
🔹 The US conducts limited follow-on strikes
🔹 Mediation brings a temporary pause
🔹 Shipping resumes, but under higher security costs
🔹 The underlying disputes remain unresolved
🔹 A new trigger event restarts the cycle

This is why the Strait of Hormuz never returns to “normal” immediately

Even after de-escalation, the risk remains embedded in the system

🔹 Higher insurance
🔹 Military escorts
🔹 Slower vessel movement
🔹 Freight volatility
🔹 Energy price risk
🔹 LNG and crude flow uncertainty

The headline may be ceasefire

But the structure is still escalation risk

Until the root causes are resolved, Hormuz remains a recurring geopolitical pressure point
